data.title: Create docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ with all 5 documentation files
data.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Context
Create all 5 documentation files that make up the stage-boundary-preconditions manifest documentation. This task makes the RED structural boundary test (T1: d7c4-5143) turn GREEN. Files cover: schema reference, validator guide, consumer guide, ops runbook, and pinned contracts index.

## TDD Requirement
No new test written (GREEN task — implements to make T1's RED tests pass). Exemption citation: Unit exemption criterion 3 — this task modifies only static documentation assets (Markdown files) where no additional executable assertion is possible beyond the structural boundary test written in T1.

## Files to Create

### 1. docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/schema-reference.md
Sections required:
- `## Overview` — purpose, placement (flat files in ticket dir), naming convention
- `## Minimal Tier` — all required fields with type, description, example JSON
- `## Standard Tier` — added fields beyond minimal (decisions_log, unresolved_questions, execution_context, committed_diff_hash, dispatch_profile)
- `## Deep Tier` — added fields beyond standard (test_contract, parser_input_classes, red_markers_active)
- `## Field Shapes` — gate_verdicts composite key, workflow_completion_checklist structure, evidence_ref structure, affects_fields
- `## schema_version Evolution Policy` — forward-compat rule (unknown versions fall back to minimal with warning), field-addition guidance
- `## p95 Latency Budgets` — placeholder section noting values are published from Story 6 benchmarks; actual values populated when 1e71-3886 completes
- `## Examples` — minimal, standard, and deep example JSON blocks

### 2. docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/validator-guide.md
Sections required:
- `## Overview` — what a stage-boundary validator does
- `## Depth-Agnostic Contract` — validators read only minimal-tier fields; ignore unknown fields; never reject well-formed standard/deep events
- `## Adding a New Validator` — step-by-step guide: entry point, _read_latest_preconditions call, field checks, zero-user-interaction invariant
- `## Schema-Roundtrip Self-Verification` — how to implement schema roundtrip check
- `## Cross-Stage Chain Verification` — using event_id references to trace chain-of-trust
- `## Zero-User-Interaction Invariant` — rule: source_type:user validates against captured comments; source_type:inference structurally accepted
- `## Testing Your Validator` — structural test template

### 3. docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/consumer-guide.md
Sections required:
- `## Overview` — what it means to consume PRECONDITIONS events
- `## _read_latest_preconditions Semantics` — how the function works (reads all PRECONDITIONS events, applies LWW)
- `## Composite-Key LWW` — latest-selection rule: highest timestamp per (stage, session_id, worktree_id); ties by filename lex order
- `## Snapshot vs Flat-Event Handling` — how to handle both formats (post-compaction snapshot and pre-compaction flat events)
- `## Legacy-Ticket Graceful-Degrade` — behavior when no PRECONDITIONS events exist (proceed without validation, log one-time notice)
- `## Consumer Integration Checklist` — steps to add a new consumer

### 4. docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ops-runbook.md
Sections required:
- `## Overview` — purpose of this runbook
- `## FP Auto-Fallback Behavior` — per-write scope, new events only, per-ticket; existing events remain honored; triggers and observability markers (log lines to look for)
- `## FP Auto-Fallback Observability` — log format: `[DSO PRECONDITIONS] FP fallback triggered: ticket=<id> rate=<N>% threshold=10%`
- `## Legacy-Ticket Handling` — tickets without PRECONDITIONS events; one-time notice log format
- `## Compaction Timing` — when compaction runs (epic-closure validator), ordering contract (validator before compaction), atomic-rename snapshot behavior
- `## Diagnostic Log Formats` — table of all log patterns with meaning
- `## Baseline vs Measurement Methodology` — how to compute pre-epic baseline, how to measure restart-rate improvement
- `## p95 Latency Budgets` — table with final benchmark values from Story 6 (1e71-3886); placeholder note if values not yet published

### 5. docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/pinned-contracts.md
Sections required:
- `## Overview` — what pinned contracts are and why they exist
- `## Contract 1: Latest-Selection + LWW` — full contract text from preplanning context
- `## Contract 2: Depth-Agnostic Validator` — full contract text
- `## Contract 3: Self-Verification Semantics` — full contract text
- `## Contract 4: schema_version Forward-Compat Policy` — full contract text
- `## Contract 5: Compaction Ordering Contract` — full contract text
- `## Contract 6: FP Auto-Fallback Scope Contract` — full contract text
- `## Invariant Enforcement` — note that these are structural contracts; violation requires modifying this file first

Contract text to use from preplanning context (PREPLANNING_CONTEXT_v1 comment on epic 736d-b957):
1. latest_selection_and_lww: "latest = highest timestamp per (stage, session_id, worktree_id); ties by filename lex order; read-side LWW across full event set"
2. depth_agnostic_validator: "validators read only minimal-tier fields; ignore unknown fields; never reject well-formed standard/deep events"
3. self_verification_semantics: "schema-roundtrip + cross-stage chain via event_id reference; chain-of-trust rooted at brainstorm writer's schema check"
4. schema_version_evolution: "every event carries schema_version; unknown versions fall back to minimal interpretation with one-time warning"
5. compaction_ordering: "epic-closure validator runs before compaction; atomic-rename snapshot; reader handles both formats; retry-once on transient miss"
6. fp_auto_fallback_scope: "per-write, new events only, per-ticket; existing standard/deep events remain honored; validators stay depth-agnostic"

## Implementation Notes
- Create the directory first: `mkdir -p docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/`
- All files are Markdown; follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` conventions
- The p95 latency budget sections should note: "Final values populated when Story 6 (1e71-3886) benchmarks complete. See ops-runbook.md for benchmark methodology."
- Use actual contract text from the epic's PREPLANNING_CONTEXT_v1 comment (above) for pinned-contracts.md

## File Impact
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/schema-reference.md` | Create |
| `docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/validator-guide.md` | Create |
| `docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/consumer-guide.md` | Create |
| `docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ops-runbook.md` | Create |
| `docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/pinned-contracts.md` | Create |

## Retry Budget
MAX_ATTEMPTS: 3 (sonnet model)
On 3 consecutive sonnet failures: escalate to opus with full diagnostic context (all 3 failure messages)
On 3 consecutive opus failures (6 total): escalate to user with full failure history
If MAX_AGENTS: 0 at sonnet→opus escalation time: skip opus step, escalate to user immediately

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 5 doc files exist in docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/
  Verify: for f in schema-reference.md validator-guide.md consumer-guide.md ops-runbook.md pinned-contracts.md; do test -f $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/$f || exit 1; done
- [ ] schema-reference.md contains all 3 tier headings (minimal, standard, deep)
  Verify: grep -q "## Minimal Tier" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/schema-reference.md && grep -q "## Standard Tier" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/schema-reference.md && grep -q "## Deep Tier" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/schema-reference.md
- [ ] pinned-contracts.md contains all 6 contract sections
  Verify: for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do grep -q "## Contract $i" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/pinned-contracts.md || exit 1; done
- [ ] ops-runbook.md contains FP auto-fallback, compaction timing, and diagnostic log sections
  Verify: grep -q "FP Auto-Fallback" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ops-runbook.md && grep -q "Compaction Timing" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ops-runbook.md && grep -q "Diagnostic Log" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/docs/designs/stage-boundary-preconditions/ops-runbook.md
- [ ] Structural boundary test (T1) passes after this task
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/docs/test-stage-boundary-preconditions-doc-structure.sh
